title: MCP server cannot start — server.run_stdio() is not a real SDK method
evidence: server.py:451 calls asyncio.run(server.run_stdio()); the mcp SDK Server class has no run_stdio(). stdio_server is imported at server.py:8 but never used. Correct pattern is `async with stdio_server() as (r,w): await server.run(r,w,server.create_initialization_options())`. main() raises AttributeError immediately.
suggestion: Wire main() through the imported stdio_server() context manager and call server.run(read, write, init_options).

title: get_team_stats W/L/D counts are wrong (double-counted from both perspectives)
evidence: repository.py:195-197 sets wins = home_wins + away_wins, draws = home_draws + away_draws, losses = home_losses + away_losses. For any match set, home_wins counts home_goal>away_goal and away_wins counts away_goal>home_goal, so wins sums decisive results from BOTH sides — inflating wins/losses and reporting every decisive match as both a win and a loss; draws are also doubled. goals_for/goals_against (repository.py:182-183) always sum home_goal/away_goal regardless of whether the team played away, so away-match goals are attributed to the wrong side.
suggestion: Split each match by whether the team was home or away and tally results/goals from that team's perspective; do not add the two opposite-perspective tallies together.

title: away_matches boolean mask indexed against a subset (index misalignment)
evidence: repository.py:175 computes away_matches = team_matches[~home_mask & team_mask], but home_mask/team_mask were built on the full df (repository.py:146-150) while team_matches is already df[team_mask]; pandas aligns on index so this is fragile and relies on implicit reindexing.
suggestion: Recompute the home/away masks against team_matches, or use team_matches.loc with a mask derived from team_matches.

title: get_top_scorers reports team goal totals, not player scorers
evidence: repository.py:338-372 aggregates home_goal/away_goal per team and returns team totals; server.py:230-233 tool is named get_top_scorers and description admits 'player-level data not available'. Misleading tool name vs. behavior.
suggestion: Rename to get_top_scoring_teams or clearly document that player-level scorers are not derivable from the match datasets.

title: Server layer is untested — 0% of server.py exercised, so the broken entrypoint is not caught
evidence: All tests (tests/test_repository.py 27 unit tests + tests/*.feature 20 BDD scenarios via tests/conftest.py) call SoccerRepository directly; server.py list_tools/call_tool/main() are never imported or invoked. test_coverage=0.56 (scores.json) reflects repository/data_loader only.
suggestion: Add a smoke test that constructs create_server() and invokes call_tool for each tool name, and a test that main() can start.

title: Dead duplicate step definitions in tests/steps/steps.py
evidence: summary/index.md and tests/steps/steps.py (11717 bytes) duplicate the active pytest-bdd step defs that live in tests/conftest.py; steps.py is never imported by the collected test modules.
suggestion: Delete tests/steps/steps.py or consolidate step definitions in one place.
